title: Button
description: A button triggers an action or event. Primary for main actions, secondary for alternatives, ghost for low-emphasis.
whenToUse: Use buttons to trigger an action (submit, save, cancel, delete). Primary for the main action; secondary for alternatives; ghost for low-emphasis.
whenNotToUse: Do not use buttons for navigation links — use an anchor (<a>) instead. Avoid more than 2–3 buttons in a row; consider a dropdown or action menu.
acceptanceCriteria: Public API uses <udc-button> Web Component tag.
Three variants render: primary (filled), secondary (outline), ghost (text-only)
Default and sm sizes render at the documented heights
Leading- and trailing-icon slots accept any Material Symbols Outlined name
Icon-only mode renders as a square; consumer must supply aria-label
All states render distinct visuals: default, hover, active, focus-visible, selected, disabled
Focus ring uses --uds-color-border-outline-focus-visible and meets WCAG 2.4.7
Disabled buttons have aria-disabled or the native disabled attribute and do not fire click
Click fires on Space release and Enter press, matching native <button> semantics

props.name: leading-icon
props.description: Material Symbols name before the label.

props.name: trailing-icon
props.description: Material Symbols name after the label.

states.name: loading
states.description: Not yet supported in 0.2 — track in component changelog

contentGuidelines: Sentence case verbs ("Save changes", "Delete tenant"). Avoid generic labels like "OK" or "Submit". Keep button text under 20 characters; if the action requires explanation, surface it in adjacent helper copy.

dos: Use sentence case verbs that describe the outcome
Pair Cancel + primary action in dialogs (Cancel on the left, primary on the right)
Reserve danger styling for genuinely destructive actions
donts: Don't use buttons for navigation — use anchor (<a>) tags
Don't put more than one primary button on a single surface
Don't gate the danger variant behind a single click without confirmation for irreversible actions
Don't rely on hover-only affordances; the focus state must be equally visible

keyboard.action: Move focus to / from the button
Activate the button on key release
Activate the button on key press

screenReader.announcement: [Label], button — announces "disabled" when the disabled attribute is present
Reads the aria-label on the <button> element; the icon itself is aria-hidden

wcag.howMet: Buttons are reachable and operable via keyboard (Tab/Shift+Tab, Space, Enter)
Visible focus ring on focus-visible using semantic outline tokens
All variants meet 4.5:1 text contrast in light, dark, and every brand
Border / focus ring meets 3:1 against adjacent surfaces

visualHierarchy: Primary buttons are the page's main action — at most one per surface. Secondary buttons sit alongside primaries (Cancel + Save). Ghost buttons are for low-emphasis actions in toolbars or nested controls.
densityBehavior: Default size is the standard target across forms and dialogs. Use size="sm" inside table cells, dense toolbars, and the comfortable density when paired with sm inputs.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Always use a <button> element, not a <div> or <span>. Provide visible text labels; icon-only buttons need aria-label. Focus ring uses --uds-color-border-outline-focus-visible.
